markdown:
May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** L --- This cloak appears to be woven from a thousand living leaves, hungry for flesh and eager to defend the cloak’s wearer. **Activate—Bite Back** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You are damaged by a melee attack from an adjacent creature; **Effect** The leaves lash out at your attacker, rising up to reveal snapping jaws made of wicked thorns. The triggering creature must attempt a DC 17 Reflex saving throw. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature takes 1d4 piercing damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d4 piercing damage and 1 [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) damage. Cloak of Gnawing Leaves **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 60 gp Cloak of Thirsty Fronds **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 330 gp --- The save DC is 23, and the cloak can be activated once per minute. The leaves deal 1d6 piercing damage to a creature on a failed save, or 2d6 piercing damage and 2 bleed damage on a critical failure. Cloak of Devouring Thorns **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230 **Price** 2,000 gp --- The save DC is 30, and the cloak can be activated once per round. The leaves deal 2d6 piercing damage to a creature on a failed save, or 4d6 piercing damage and 3 bleed damage on a critical failure.
name: Cloak of Devouring Thorns

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stalk Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4024)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Morph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=113" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 230

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These black leather goggle frames have no lenses. Instead, when a character puts them on and invests them, the wearer’s eyes transform and lengthen into snail-like eyestalks. The wobbly eyestalks stretch out through the lens holes and up over the wearer’s head on lengthened optic nerves.

**Activate—Looksee** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** By focusing hard, you can watch for enemies in all directions. You gain [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) for 1 minute; during this time, you can’t be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

**Price** 20 gp

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the goggles, you also gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks involving sight for the duration of the effect.

level="2"
right="Item 8"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Stalk Goggles
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 231

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

When you activate the goggles, you also gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks involving sight and [low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2411) for the duration of the effect
name: Stalk Goggles

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Bottled Omen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4025)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 231

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This potion holds a tiny, tightly wrapped scroll and tastes like paper. Upon drinking it, you gain a burst of insight into your immediate future—and how to potentially avoid it. When you attempt a saving throw, you can roll twice and use the better result. The potion’s magic ends when you make use of this effect, or after 1 minute. You then become immune to _bottled omen_ potions for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bottled Omen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Draft of Stellar Radiance](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4026)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 231

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This potion’s bottle glows softly with shimmering silver light. Upon drinking this potion, you’re surrounded by a nimbus of blazing starlight that lasts for 1 minute. You emanate a field of bright light with a 20-foot radius (and dim light for another 20 feet, like a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760)). You take a –20 penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks. Any creature that targets you with an attack or an ability must succeed at a DC 17 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. A creature who succeeds at this save is immune to the effect for 24 hours.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Draft of Stellar Radiance

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4027)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 232, [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 34

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>**-Calculate** 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus to checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe functions only on steady ground.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 2"
pfs="Limited"
>
Standard Astrolabe
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Mariner's Astrolabe
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 232

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A mariner’s astrolabe contains additional stabilizers that make it suitable for use on moving surfaces, such as the deck of a ship.
name: Astrolabe

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>**-Calculate** 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Astrolabes can be used for navigation in unfamiliar or featureless locations. To use an astrolabe, the holder must be trained in [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49). By spending 1 minute to measure the height of the stars and planets, a holder who knows the time and date can determine the latitude, and a holder who knows their latitude can determine the date and time. An astrolabe also grants a +1 item bonus to checks to identify celestial bodies. A standard astrolabe functions only on steady ground.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Astrolabe of Falling Stars](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4028)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 232

**Price** 150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2411) is fashioned of magically attuned platinum plates and rings and is inset with carefully measured ruby gems. The astrolabe’s back is engraved with helpful charts, a calendar, and a maker’s mark. 

An _astrolabe of falling stars_ can be used as a mariner’s astrolabe. In addition, it can produce the following magical effect.

**Activate—Starfall** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** With a flick of your finger, you set the astrolabe’s rule spinning, causing the inset rubies to become a blur. Choose a point within 100 feet. The astrolabe of falling stars calls down a brief rain of meteorites in a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) centered on that point, dealing 1d8 bludgeoning and 1d8 fire damage to creatures in the area (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Astrolabe of Falling Stars

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stargazer's Spyglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4029)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 232

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ornate spyglass has a brass tube inscribed with constellations and green-tinted lens. It’s often used by field astronomers to pick out greater details among stars and other celestial bodies. Like a typical spyglass, you can see eight times farther while looking through a _stargazer’s spyglass._ 

**Activate—Twinkle** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You set your eye upon a star and think of someone to cast [_guiding star_](/Spells.aspx?ID=923).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stargazer's Spyglass

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Aether Marbles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4032)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 235

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Strike</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Aether marbles create violent, lingering eddies of force that extend partially into unseen planes. When the bomb explodes, it deals the listed [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) damage and force splash damage. Many aether marbles grant an item bonus to attack rolls.

Surfaces in the splash area are filled with twisting eddies of force, which are [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures but not for corporeal ones. The force eddies in a square disperse after an incorporeal creature enters the square.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls. The bomb deals 4d6 force damage and 4 force splash damage.
name: Aether Marbles

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Telekinetic Converters](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4033)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 235

**Price** 1,950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Copper cables run from these thick leather gloves to nodes that attach to the wearer’s temples, allowing a spellcaster to convert the magic they normally cast to pure psychic intention. When you Cast a Spell using the _telekinetic converters_, you substitute any spoken words for simple thoughts, granting the [subtle](/Traits.aspx?ID=522) trait to the spell you’re casting.

**Activate—Mental Fling** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** You have a spellcasting class feature and you can cast cantrips; **Effect** You cast [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) as an [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) [cantrip](/Traits.aspx?ID=550), heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

**Activate—Over Matter** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))   **Requirements** You have a spellcasting class feature and have an unexpended spell slot of 5th rank or higher; **Effect** You cast your choice of [_telekinetic haul_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1716) or [_telekinetic maneuver_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) as a 5th-rank occult spell, consuming one of your unexpended spell slots of the same rank as if you had used it to cast the spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Telekinetic Converters

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Psychic Colors Elixir](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4034)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=59" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 235

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This orange liquid lets you visibly detect the psychic energies that enable telekinesis and telepathy. For the next minute, you can sense if a creature you can see is using a telepathic or telekinetic ability, such as the [telepathy monster ability](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79), a spell like [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718), or similar abilities. You also can sense if an object or creature you can see is being manipulated or contacted by such an ability. Both the user and the target of the ability are outlined in faint shimmers of matching color.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Psychic Colors Elixir

A malformed, artificial brain pulses with alchemical life inside a nutrient-rich vat. When the vat-grown brain is activated, it attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) one condition of your choice that was gained from an ability with the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) trait, which it does by drawing the negative mental impressions into itself. However, the artificial brain is not robust, and the strain of the transfer quickly destroys it. The vat-grown brain has a counteract level of 5 and a +17 modifier on the roll.

    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mindsponge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4036)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 235

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _mindsponge_ appears as a misshapen gray sponge that unsettlingly resembles a malformed human brain. Though pliable in all the same ways as a damp sponge, a _mindsponge_ has no ability to absorb water and, in fact, doesn’t seem terribly porous at all. Instead, a _mindsponge_ is used to absorb thoughts and memories from living creatures.

**Activate—Wipe Memories** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The _mindsponge_ casts [_modify memory_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655), but only to erase a memory—a _mindsponge_ can’t enhance memory clarity, alter memories, or add false memories.

**Activate—Absorb Memories** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You use the _mindsponge_ to harvest the memories and mental energy of a specially prepared target as it dies. To prepare the target (who must be willing or helpless), you must perform a 10-minute surgery to expose a portion of the target’s brain without killing them. This requires a successful DC 28 [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) (master) check; if you fail this check, the target dies before you can use the _mindsponge_.

Memories and psychic energy absorbed aren’t taken from the target’s soul but from energies temporarily infused within the body, so that a creature slain in this way who is later brought back to life retains their harvested memories and energy. The corpse of someone who has had their memories harvested is difficult to communicate with via [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712)—treat the saving throw result against that spell as one degree of success lower than the actual result. 

A _mindsponge_ can store the memories and energy of up to four creatures at a time. Once full, it cannot be activated at all, other than to squeeze it as an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action with the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, which causes all of the memories within to drip out as cold ectoplasm, which swiftly evaporates if not used or contained.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mindsponge

[Ice Forge](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4037)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Gatewalkers</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Structure" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=153" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 237

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L When not activated
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While many of the magical wonders created by saumen kar have been destroyed, _ice forges_, while rare, still see use today among the few individuals who manage to discover and keep one of these portable treasures. When an _ice forge_ isn’t activated, it appears to be a small anvil carved from a lump of ice no larger than an apple. While in this state, the _ice forge_ remains cold to the touch but doesn’t melt in the presence of high temperatures. It can be used to chill a beverage in a small container, although those who venerate and respect these items’ legacy would doubtless find this use to be insulting at best.

**Activate—Expand Forge** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You toss the _ice forge_ onto the ground, and it immediately expands in size, transforming into a fully functional forge that appears to be made of solid ice. Expanded, the _ice forge_ is 10 feet square and 10 feet high. While the forge itself is cold to the touch, it won’t melt in higher temperatures, nor will it cause harm to someone who remains in contact with the chill surface for an extended period of time. The forge can be used to work metal as a normal forge (although fuel must be provided)—the heat created by its use does no harm to the ice forge, nor does it transmit to the surface it sits upon, making an ice forge an ideal solution for those who seek to use forges in areas where open flames or smoke would be dangerous.

You can return an _ice forge_ to its handheld anvil form by spending a single action to issue a verbal command, which has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait. Doing so immediately snuffs out any fire within the forge and leaves behind unused material or ashes. Once deactivated, the forge can’t be activated again for 4 hours.

**Activate—Ice Sculpture** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** The _ice forge_ is in forge form; **Effect** The ice forge casts [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477) (heightened to 5th rank) to your specifications.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ice Forge

[Adlet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1054) spiritual leaders use this thick purple gel during ancestral worship ceremonies, applying it that the dead so that their bodies can temporarily assume the appearance they had in life. Spreading it over the bones of an undead creature or a lifeless corpse causes the gel to congeal, forming a cosmetic layer that covers or restores any missing or compromised flesh until the body mimics its appearance in life. The dead creature’s flesh looks healthy and whole. An undead creature covered with the oil gains a +2 circumstance bonus on [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to look like a living creature. The gel does not restore life or Hit Points and the flesh quickly rots away 8 hours after application.

Magical reagents slosh inside this rime‑frosted pottery jar the size of a human head. Despite appearing to be rimed in hoarfrost, the jar is warm to the touch. Ilverani wizards create _rime jars_ to sustain themselves during the coldest winter nights.

**Activate—Siphon Chill** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The jar draws off the cold and warms your body. If you have the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition caused by exposure to [environmental cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820), it removes the condition. For 8 hours after applying the rime, you treat extreme cold as severe cold.

Found throughout Golarion, these star-shaped talismans of whalebone scrimshaw are carved by the Erutaki people from the Crown of the World. They are popular with adherents to the cult of [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281), who believe the talismans protect their dreams.

When you activate this talisman, you gain a +2 status bonus to saves against spells with the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) trait for 1 minute. On the triggering save, if the outcome of the roll is a failure, you get a success instead, or if the outcome is a critical failure, you get a failure instead.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs=""
>
[Paradigm Cube](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4041)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 70

**Price** 450 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _paradigm cube_ is a perfect 5-inch-square cube of stone. Each side is inlaid with precious crystals of a different color: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and indigo. When one activates a _paradigm cube_, one of these sides is selected—it glows with the matching color and then darkens to a shade of violet once the activation ends, indicating that the magic from that particular side of the cube has been expended for the day. 

**Activate—Change Paradgm** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** six times per day (but only once per day per color);   **Effect** Select one of the six colors of the cube that hasn’t been activated today, then roll the cube on the ground at your feet and attempt a DC 11 flat check. On a success, your chosen color activates as detailed below. On a failure, roll 1d6 to randomly determine which effect manifests; if the result of the roll is a color that’s already been activated today, no effect occurs. The paradigm cube then immediately returns to your hand. If you spend 1 Mythic Point as part of this activation, your chosen color automatically activates without requiring a flat check, and the chosen effect’s mythic enhancement unlocks. Once activated, the illusion created fills a 60-foot emanation from you and lasts for 1 minute. You can end the effect early by activating Change Paradigm again by tapping the currently active color.

**1—Red** (emotion, mental, visual) Red is the color of anger and impatience. A crimson haze covers the area, setting everyone within the area on edge. Allies in the area gain a +2 item bonus to Intimidation checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394). **Mythic Enhancement:** Allies’ saving throws against mental and [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) effects are attempted at mythic proficiency.

**2—Orange** (light, visual) Orange is the color of glory and opulence. The area becomes bedecked in elegant images, glamorous decor, and spectacular displays, and becomes brightly lit. Allies in the area gain a +2 item bonus to Performance checks, and allies who [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=2802) in the area gain an additional 3 temporary Hit Points above what they would normally gain. **Mythic Enhancement:** Allies’ Perception checks and Diplomacy checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) are attempted at mythic proficiency.

**3—Yellow** (emotion, fear, light, mental, visual) Yellow is the color of terror and discord. The area’s illumination, if any, takes on an unnerving, murky yellow tinge and becomes dim light. Allies in the area gain a +2 item bonus to Intimidation checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). **Mythic Enhancement:** Enemy creatures in the area who gain the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as long as they remain frightened, as the yellow light seems to cling to them and slow their movements to a nightmarish crawl.

**4—Green** (revelation, visual) Green is the color of logic and clarity. Visible text in the area becomes softly illuminated when read. Allies in the area gain a +2 item bonus to Lore checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367). **Mythic Enhancement:** Attempts made by allies to counteract or disbelieve illusions or to save against confusion effects are attempted at mythic proficiency.

**5—Blue** (emotion, light, mental, visual) Blue is the color of debilitating sadness and fractures in one’s emotional facade. Somber light replaces the normal lighting, filling the area with dim illumination, and moods darken. Allies in the area gain a +2 item bonus to Perception checks to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302). **Mythic Enhancement:** An enemy in the area that fails an attack roll, saving throw, or skill check becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 from sadness until the end of their next turn (stupefied 2 on a critical failure).

**6—Indigo** (darkness, shadow) Indigo is the color of ignorance and the unknown. The area becomes cloaked in darkness, as per the darkness spell heightened to 6th rank. Allies in the area gain a +2 item bonus to Stealth checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404). **Mythic Enhancement:** Allies’ saving throws against fear, curse, or visual effects are attempted at mythic proficiency.

**Craft Requirements** You must be a mythic arcane spellcaster capable of attempting Crafting checks at mythic proficiency.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Paradigm Cube

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs=""
>
[Sihedron Medallion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4043)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 69

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These amulets once served as tokens of loyalty between the runelords and their favored agents. Though they vary in appearance, they all share a common design—a leather or metallic cord attached to a metal disc inscribed with the seven-pointed star known as the Sihedron. For original _Sihedron medallions_ created before Earthfall, a runelord of old Thassilon (not a character with the runelord archetype) doesn’t reduce the DC of any [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) spell they cast against a creature that wears a Sihedron medallion even if they have never met the target or are unaware of the target’s identity. If such a runelord successfully scries on a creature wearing an original _Sihedron medallion_, they can use the wearer’s voice to issue messages to those in the vicinity. _Sihedron medallions_ created in more modern times don’t possess this disadvantage.

A dead body that wears a _Sihedron medallion_ doesn’t decay, and bugs or other pests (such as maggots) are prevented from consuming the remains, but unlike the effects of [_peaceful rest_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625), this doesn’t offer any protection against undeath, nor does it have an impact on spells that require the corpse to have died within a certain amount of time. 

**Activate—Bolster Flesh** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your flesh is augmented with magical vitality. You gain 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for 8 hours.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs=""
>
[Sihedron Medallion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4043)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 69

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These amulets once served as tokens of loyalty between the runelords and their favored agents. Though they vary in appearance, they all share a common design—a leather or metallic cord attached to a metal disc inscribed with the seven-pointed star known as the Sihedron. For original _Sihedron medallions_ created before Earthfall, a runelord of old Thassilon (not a character with the runelord archetype) doesn’t reduce the DC of any [_scrying_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1662) spell they cast against a creature that wears a Sihedron medallion even if they have never met the target or are unaware of the target’s identity. If such a runelord successfully scries on a creature wearing an original _Sihedron medallion_, they can use the wearer’s voice to issue messages to those in the vicinity. _Sihedron medallions_ created in more modern times don’t possess this disadvantage.

A dead body that wears a _Sihedron medallion_ doesn’t decay, and bugs or other pests (such as maggots) are prevented from consuming the remains, but unlike the effects of [_peaceful rest_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1625), this doesn’t offer any protection against undeath, nor does it have an impact on spells that require the corpse to have died within a certain amount of time. 

**Activate—Bolster Flesh** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Your flesh is augmented with magical vitality. You gain 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for 8 hours.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"

>
[Sihedron Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4044)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 69

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though simple in appearance, _Sihedron rings_ are among the most treasured tokens a runelord would bestow on an agent in times past. Like the more common _Sihedron medallion_, _Sihedron rings_ created by the original runelords allow for easier scrying of and speaking through those who wear them; _Sihedron rings_ created in modern times don’t have this disadvantage.

As long as it’s invested, a _Sihedron ring_ protects its wearer from environmental extremes, rendering them immune to severe cold and heat. The ring also grants a +1 status bonus to AC against attacks made during reactions. 

**Activate—Costume Change** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))    **Effect** You change the shape and appearance of any armor or clothing you wear to appear as ordinary or fancy clothing or armor of your imagining. The actual statistics for what you’re wearing doesn’t change. Only a creature that’s benefiting from [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) or a similar effect can attempt to disbelieve this illusion, with a DC of 20.

</column>

</column>

---

The ring protects from extreme cold or heat and grants a +2 status bonus to AC against attacks made during reactions. Costume Change becomes a free action, with a DC of 29.

<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 70

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Runelord Xanderghul often crafted _Cyrusian wands_ to bolster his access to his favorite illusion spells. All _Cyrusian wands_ look different but equally audacious in their coloration, shape, and glittering gemstone adornments.

A _Cyrusian wand_ only ever contains an arcane spell with the illusion trait. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank. If you spend 1 Mythic Point as part of this activation, resolve the spell’s effects at mythic proficiency. 

**Activate—Presume Success** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail the DC 10 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) when you overcharge the wand;  **Effect** You disbelieve your failure, willing the check to success. Spend 1 Mythic Point and reroll the flat check, then resolve the spell’s effects at mythic proficiency.

**Craft Requirements** You must be a mythic arcane spellcaster capable of supplying a casting of the spell at the listed rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs=""
>
[Cyrusian Wand](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4045)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 70

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Runelord Xanderghul often crafted _Cyrusian wands_ to bolster his access to his favorite illusion spells. All _Cyrusian wands_ look different but equally audacious in their coloration, shape, and glittering gemstone adornments.

A _Cyrusian wand_ only ever contains an arcane spell with the illusion trait. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank. If you spend 1 Mythic Point as part of this activation, resolve the spell’s effects at mythic proficiency. 

**Activate—Presume Success** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail the DC 10 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) when you overcharge the wand;  **Effect** You disbelieve your failure, willing the check to success. Spend 1 Mythic Point and reroll the flat check, then resolve the spell’s effects at mythic proficiency.

**Craft Requirements** You must be a mythic arcane spellcaster capable of supplying a casting of the spell at the listed rank.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs=""
>
[Cyrusian Wand](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4045)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=164" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 70

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Runelord Xanderghul often crafted _Cyrusian wands_ to bolster his access to his favorite illusion spells. All _Cyrusian wands_ look different but equally audacious in their coloration, shape, and glittering gemstone adornments.

A _Cyrusian wand_ only ever contains an arcane spell with the illusion trait. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You Cast the Spell at the indicated rank. If you spend 1 Mythic Point as part of this activation, resolve the spell’s effects at mythic proficiency. 

**Activate—Presume Success** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail the DC 10 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) when you overcharge the wand;  **Effect** You disbelieve your failure, willing the check to success. Spend 1 Mythic Point and reroll the flat check, then resolve the spell’s effects at mythic proficiency.

**Craft Requirements** You must be a mythic arcane spellcaster capable of supplying a casting of the spell at the listed rank.

    right="Item 13"
    pfs=""
>
[Hollow Robes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4046)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Revenge of the Runelords</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Mythic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=835" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 70

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These heavy cloth garments function as a set of +2 _[resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [ explorer’s clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715)_. They feature multiple stylish layers, voluminous sleeves, and long strings of Thassilonian runes along the hems. The robes employ potent illusion magic, sufficient to convince the wearer and any attackers that a devastating blow is nothing more than a flesh wound. 

<document level="2" id="action-3523" /> 

<document level="2" id="action-3524" /> 

<document level="2" id="action-3525" />

**Craft Requirements** You must be a mythic arcane spellcaster capable of casting [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hollow Robes

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[Flawless Celestial Shawl](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4048)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=537" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 27

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Each _flawless celestial shawl_ functions only for a specific tennin and is the culmination of their immortal cultivation. The sash is made of celestial silken threads stronger than [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) yet lighter than gossamer. The tennin can draw thread from the _flawless celestial shawl_ to serve as a [sterling artisan’s toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2703) and workshop to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385). The sash can be used to work with [precious materials](/Rules.aspx?ID=3190) of any grade. On any [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check the tennin attempts using the shawl, they use the outcome one degree of success better than the result of their roll.

**Activate—Reweave** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ;     **Effect** The tennin uses the sash to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) an item. This Repair activity loses the [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Flawless Celestial Shawl

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Energizing Pill](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4055)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Lozenge" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=484" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 295

**Price** 180 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This special blend of potent herbs comes in the form of a compact ball the size of a marble that allows it to be held in the mouth, where it remains for 1 hour. An energizing pill has a bitter taste at first, but the longer one lets it soak, the more complex and sweet its flavor grows. It heightens your reactions, granting you a +2 item bonus to initiative rolls with Perception.

**Secondary Effect** <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** You would become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94); **Effect** Reduce the value of the condition you just gained by 1; if this reduces the value to 0, you don’t gain the triggering condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Energizing Pill

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Invigorating Soap](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4056)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Processed" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=487" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 295

**Price** 28 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="" /><row>10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This slender bar of pine-scented soap can only be activated when you’re immersed in water. Upon activation, the soap covers you in a sudsy foam that quickly fades, filling you with energy and soothing away aches and pains. It immediately restores 10 Hit Points and removes the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition. If you begin an 8-hour period of rest immediately after using invigorating soap, you regain an additional 10 Hit Points from resting.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Invigorating Soap

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Matsuki's Medicinal Wine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4057)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 295

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Old Matsuki’s private brew has quite the kick. In addition to making you tipsy, this medicinal wine works wonders in helping to recover from [disease](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767). When you drink a dose of this wine you must succeed at a DC 18 Fortitude save to avoid becoming [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 10 minutes (and also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for this duration if you critically fail the saving throw). Regardless of the saving throw’s results, you gain a +2 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws against diseases and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) for the next 24 hours. This applies to your daily save against a disease’s progression as well.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Matsuki's Medicinal Wine

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Silversoul Bomb](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4058)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Bomb" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=21" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Splash" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=150" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 295

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>[Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306)</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rare alchemical bomb gathers and concentrates the emotions that linger in a burial ground where generations of beloved ancestors have been interred, infusing that powerful emotional energy into the alchemically prepared powdered [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) stored within the bomb. This energy glows with a soft, silver radiance and, if stored in a clear container, allows a silversoul bomb to be used as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760) to illuminate an area.

A silversoul bomb grants an item bonus to attack rolls and deals mental damage plus additional effects, according to the bomb’s type. [Nindorus](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=353) are particularly harmed by silversoul bombs and take the listed persistent mental damage, according to the bomb’s type. Against nindorus, the bomb’s item bonus also applies to its save DC to resist being [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59). Creatures that have [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) to silver (including most nindorus) have an equal amount of weakness to the mental damage caused by a silversoul bomb, due to the silver infused into the energy within.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Limited"
>
Silversoul Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296

**Price** 10 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +1 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 2d4 mental damage, 1d6 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) to nindorus, and 2 mental [splash](/Traits.aspx?ID=699) damage. A creature that takes splash damage from the bomb and fails a DC 17 Fortitude save is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round as glowing silver particles cling to its face.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Limited"
>
Greater Silversoul Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +2 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 3d4 mental damage, 2d6 persistent mental damage to nindorus, and 3 mental splash damage. A creature that takes splash damage from the bomb and fails a DC 28 Fortitude save is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round and then dazzled for 1 round thereafter.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Limited"
>
Major Silversoul Bomb
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296

**Price** 2,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You gain a +3 item bonus to attack rolls, and the bomb deals 4d4 mental damage, 3d6 persistent mental damage to nindorus, and 4 mental splash damage. A creature that takes splash damage from the bomb is dazzled for 1 round unless it fails a DC 37 Fortitude save, in which case it’s permanently blinded.
name: Silversoul Bomb

While wearing this armor, you aren’t [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) from creatures that [flank](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) you, and you ignore the armor’s Speed penalty when you [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) and its check penalty to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to Swim.

    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Dragonfire Halfbow](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4060)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Composite Shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafted by layering bamboo with strips of sliced scales harvested from a fire-breathing [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395), this bow has a significant draw strength. This design is commonly seen in Hongal, as the shorter limb allows for easier maneuvering on horseback. While the wielder of this _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [composite shortbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=435)_ is [mounted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2434), they apply the bow’s item bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) checks to [Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) their mount.

**Activate—Dragon's Arrow** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw the bow back to its maximum draw length without nocking an arrow, and as you release the string, the dragonfire halfbow casts [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530) (heightened to 5th rank; DC 29 Reflex save), targeted at a point of your choosing within the spell’s range. You and your mount are immune to the effects of this _fireball_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dragonfire Halfbow

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Evil-Reflecting Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4061)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A bright, octagonal frame surrounds this circular [dawnsilver shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2814), which itself is polished to a mirrorlike sheen that reflects clear and true. While you have this standard-grade dawnsilver shield ([Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 5, HP 20 [BT 10]) raised, you gain its circumstance bonus to saving throws against spells with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) trait. 

**Activate—Reflect Evil** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You attempt a saving throw against an effect with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) or [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) traits;  **Effect** Your saving throw result is considered one degree better than the actual result. If this effect turns a success into a critical success, you can reflect some of the effect’s power as a wave of magical backlash back to the source of the triggering effect, as long as the source is within 30 feet. When you do so, the target must succeed at a DC 28 Fortitude save or become [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Evil-Reflecting Shield

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296 **Price** 100 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) --- The blade of this _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [low-grade silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920) [dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358)_ has a sickly red tinge. Though once used to send souls to empower [Kugaptee](/Deities.aspx?ID=725), the fury of those slain by the blade now allows its wielder to periodically strike back against [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600). **Activate—Avenging Soul** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You score a critical hit against a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600); **Effect** Vengeful echoes of sacrificed souls lance into the psyche of the fiend struck. After the normal effects of the critical hit, the target must roll a DC 18 Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes distracted by vengeful spirits and becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.
**Failure** As success, but the creature is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is slowed 1 for 1 minute.
name: Fiend's Hunger

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 296 **Price** 2,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 **Base Weapon** [Longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386) --- When a once-in-a-lifetime alignment of the stars shone through the roof of a secluded blacksmith’s forge, the smith took advantage to craft their masterpiece. For two hours, they hammered a lump of ordinary steel into a divine blade, etching constellations and wards down its length, then shared their creation’s formula with a small group of close associates before passing away. These weapons are the _four-tiger blades_, and currently, only a dozen are known to exist—one associated with each month of the year. Each weapon is a _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=386)_ that leaves a noticeable trail of light originating from the engraved stars when it’s swung. Those who pay close attention to the pattern of stars that shine on the blade might note that the depicted constellations change according to those currently in the night sky above. **Activate—Ghost Blocking Tiger** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Trigger** You attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the _four-tiger-blade_; **Effect** Until the start of your next turn, you gain void [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10. **Activate—Ghost Chasing Tiger** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Attempt a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against a creature you believe to be possessed. This attack deals no damage to the creature, but if the target is possessed, the possessing creature must attempt a DC 29 Will save. **Critical Success** The possessing creature is unaffected, and you remain unaware of if the target is actually possessed or not.
**Success** The possessing creature is unaffected, but you sense that creature’s control over the target and confirm that the target is possessed.
**Failure** You confirm that the target is possessed. The possessing creature can’t control the target for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** You confirm the target is possessed an instant before you automatically counteract the possession, causing the possessing creature to vacate the target’s body.
name: Four-Tiger Blade

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Lumber Lord's Axe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4064)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 297

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Battle Axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=371)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This unassuming tool wouldn’t look out of place on the belt of an industrious laborer. Grooves worn into the wooden handle over years of use and an irregularly sharpened blade give it the distinct impression of being cherished, and the potent scent of freshly chopped wood always clings to the axe’s blade despite any attempt to clean it or remove the odor. A creature that holds or carries this _[+2 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=371)_ feels an obligation to tell the truth and receives a –4 status penalty to its attempts to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389). A lumber lord’s axe deals 1d6 additional slashing damage to creatures with the [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614) or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) trait, as long as the creatures aren’t disguised as non-fungus or non-plant creatures. It’s up to GM discretion whether this additional damage applies against a creature disguised as a fungus or plant creature.

**Activate—Transformative Polish** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You polish and sharpen the axe, after which its scent grows even more powerful and the axe’s blade transforms into your choice of [standard-grade adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) or [standard-grade dawnsilver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2917) for 1 hour; then after this duration, the woodcutter’s axe reverts to [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Lumber Lord's Axe

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Sensei's Parasol](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4065)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 297

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Shield** [Steel Shield](/Shields.aspx?ID=19)

**Base Weapon** [Bo Staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A master once said to his pupil that, in the hands of a skilled practitioner, even a parasol can become a deadly weapon. Returning the next day, the pupil excitedly turned over a parasol forged out of metal slats, explaining that it lowered the amount of skill one would need to exert deadly force. The master sighed, seeing that his pupil had failed to see the point, but accepted the gift nonetheless. Ingenuity was, after all, a virtue. 

Since then, other smiths have imitated the pupil’s design and crafted additional sensei’s parasols, and they’re highly sought after by those who appreciate multipurpose tools. This weapon functions as a _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372)_.

**Activate—Your Attacks Are but Raindrops** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cause the metal slats of the parasol to open up, transforming it into a [moderate sturdy shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2828) (Hardness 13, HP 104 [BT 51]). You can [sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation for up to 10 minutes, after which the sensei’s parasol reverts back to bo staff form. If it’s broken as a shield, it reverts to its undamaged bo staff form. If it’s destroyed as a shield, it reverts to bo staff form and becomes broken.

**Activate—Defensive Redirection** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature misses you with a melee [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306); **Requirements** The _sensei’s parasol_ is in [bo staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=372) form and isn’t [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60); **Effect** You redirect the missed Strike to target a creature or object within the triggering creature’s reach. The triggering creature rolls a new [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) against the new target to determine the results of the redirected attack.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sensei's Parasol

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />

</row>

<row gap="medium">

---

This massive, curved blade roars when it crashes down on opponents, seemingly intent on subduing them with sheer force. It’s said materials for the first thundering fury dadaos came from the hide of Yorak, the Horned Thunder, a legendary kaiju that roams the Shanguang desert. The lack of embellishments on this _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [thundering ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850) [ greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=379)_ belies a weapon of deadly efficacy.

**Activate—Thunder Dance** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You infuse the _thundering fury dadao_ with the speed of lightning and the force of thunder. The weapon gains the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) and [forceful](/Traits.aspx?ID=611) traits for one round. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this activation for up to 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thundering Fury Dadao

<traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

This spiky, stony fragment shed from Ugly Cute’s carapace fits comfortably over the hand. Though a little bulkier than the typical gauntlet, it still functions as a _[+1 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [spiked gauntlet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=366)_.

**Activate—Ugly Cute's Favor** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You draw upon Ugly Cute’s latent spiritual energy to infuse the gauntlet with forceful power. For 1 minute, _Ugly Cute’s gift_ gains the advantages of a _[ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840)_ property rune and deals an additional 2 spirit damage on a successful Strike.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ugly Cute's Gift

Drinking it imparts an intriguing balance of woodsmoke, honey, and bright floral top notes. When consumed, your arms become infused with the deadly power of a dragon’s restless spirit. For 1 minute, your unarmed Strikes deal an additional 1d6 poison damage and an additional 1d6 void damage.

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

---

This pungent herbal ointment was originally only intended for use as an analgesic. That being said, popular folklore around this salve claims it can do anything from curing fevers to repelling vampires, a reputation that, if nothing else, is easy to get swept up in. When you activate a dose of _panaceatic salve_ by rubbing it on your skin, you gain 4d8+10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for 10 minutes. At the end of this duration, if you still retain at least 1 of these temporary Hit Points, you can attempt a new saving throw against an ongoing [disease](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) affliction you’re afflicted with; if you fail or critically fail this saving throw, you don’t increase the affliction’s stage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Panaceatic Salve

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 298 **Price** 250 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) --- These roasted soybeans are blessed by a priest to ward against all various forms of otherworldly influence, but are most effective against [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) evils, such as [wraiths](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=164) or [path maidens](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2805). They can be safely eaten by living creatures and taste delicious when paired with rice wine. When you activate this item, choose an adjacent square to exhale into. You fill that square with soothing vapors that harm any [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600), [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599), [ghost](/Traits.aspx?ID=753), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature that ends their turn in that square for the next minute, causing them to take 4d8+4 spirit damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). An incorporeal creature that takes this damage must also attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save to resist being affected as follows. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected
**Success** The creature becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.
**Failure** The creature becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1 and is then slowed 1 for 1 round after the stun effect ends.
name: Sanctified Beans

May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts **Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 298 **Price** 5 gp **Bulk** L --- A _spirit trap_ consists of a net dipped in water that has been steeped with sacred herbs to better combat phantoms. This snare’s components function as a net when not set up. You set this snare up in a 10-foot-by-10-foot area. The first creature with the [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) trait that steps into the area must attempt a DC 16 Fortitude save. [Additional rules for snares appear here](/Rules.aspx?ID=3315). **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is immobilized until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 16).

---

The bright red tassels of the creature known as [Taljjae](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1740) are a ubiquitous motif in Hwanggot and are used as charms to pray for good fortune and reward for great effort. They adorn clothing, weapons, and even drinking gourds. These particular tassels carry within them a potent fraction of the mysterious fey’s power. When you activate a _Taljjae tassel_, your weapon gains the properties of the [_grievous_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2841) rune for the duration of the triggering attack.

</title>

---

Dragonroots are pale, golden-rooted plants that grow throughout Tian Xia. Just as mandrake roots or ginseng roots are sometimes regarded to have almost humanoid shapes, these roots appear as if they were dragons. They’re commonly used in the formulation of healing potions, but the most sought-after have grown for centuries, for they’re viable for enchantment into _thousand-year dragonroots_.

When you activate a _thousand-year dragonroot_, your body shimmers with golden light, providing illumination as a [torch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2760). You gain a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 40 feet and fire [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Thousand-Year Dragonroot

_Vengeful demon’s tears_ is an infamously noxious spirit mixed into a potent magical cocktail and traditionally packaged in a gourd adorned with an engraving of a terrifying demonic face. Drinking it feels akin to lighting your throat on fire and putting it out with an herbal tonic. When you activate this potion, you gain the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) condition for 3 rounds and can use the extra action each round to [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), or to take the following action.

**Activate—Release the Demon** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623))    **Effect** You focus on the sensation of life burning within your flesh and gain 2d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) for 1 round.

</column>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Season of Ghosts</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />

---

This green tea is often steeped in a pot with a dried persimmon. Traditionally, all participants in a complex discussion start their conversation with a tea ceremony involving _covenant tea_, with each member of the group enjoying the covenant of the shared beverage. Some have been known to use trickery and sleight of hand to ensure that only those whose goals align with the tea preparer’s are served covenant tea, while others in the group are served non-magical (but still delicious) green tea, thus subtly tipping the balance in discussion toward those the tea server favors. This practice has resulted in some regions referring to _covenant tea_ as “trickery tea.” Regardless of what you prefer to call the tea, when you drink it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks and to your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC for 10 minutes.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony**10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration increases to 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Covenant Tea

This sweet and refreshing tea is made with a mixture of honey, lemon slices, and sliced ginger. This is considered to be the bare minimum when serving _energizing tea_, and many go above and beyond by adding additional citrus fruits or berries to the mix or refusing to serve the tea at all without also serving a full platter of spiced pastries and sweets. This golden tea has energizing properties and, when consumed, grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) and [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks for 10 minutes.

---

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=130" />
<trait label="Tea" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=738" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Season of Ghosts (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=294) pg. 300

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tea is brewed with a mixture of matcha, turmeric, and 10 drops of morning dew, creating a fragrant but mildly bitter tea. The bitter aftertaste is said to be the most important element of _longnight tea_, for it’s believed that this flavor lingers on the tongue and aids in keeping you awake and alert. The tea is favored by scholars and soldiers alike, although the value to which each attributes the ability to endure long nights without sleeping varies— and is often the subject of unusually impassioned debate. When you drink _longnight tea_, it grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against sleep effects for 1 hour. 

---

Activate—Tea Ceremony 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration increases to 8 hours, and the tea removes the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) condition.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Longnight Tea

---

This bright and citrusy tea is traditionally made with green tea combined with orange rind shavings and flower petals plucked during a full moon. Some claim to be able to tell the difference if these petals were plucked at other times, but this does not alter the tea’s effects. The tea leaves have a chance to anticipate unexpected encounters. After consuming the tea, you gain a +2 item bonus to all initiative rolls for 1 hour and gain the following reaction.

**Activate—Evade Peril** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612))   **Requirements** You roll a failure on a Reflex save; **Effect** You get a success on the Reflex save instead. Reduce the remaining duration of moon blossom tea by 1 hour; if this reduces the duration to 0, the duration ends.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration increases to 4 hours.

</column>

This creamy tea is made from warm soy milk steeped with whole, dried chrysanthemum flowers and honey and brewed from when the sun first touches the horizon to when it has fully set. Consuming this tea causes mild drowsiness, aids in sleep, and protects from harmful mental effects. You gain a +1 item bonus to all saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects for 1 hour.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The effects last for 8 hours, and as long as you’re asleep, the item bonus increases to +2.

---

_Resurrection tea_ was named for its repugnant taste, said to be “strong enough to kill the living,” and its incredible medicinal properties “powerful enough to raise the dead.” The primary ingredients in this medicinal tea are ginseng and jujubes, enhanced with additional herbs, shaved deer antlers, sugi tree bark, and a variety of mushrooms stewed for several hours in a large pot to form a brown, pulpy, and viscous liquid. The bitterness and earthy taste make the tea rather unappetizing. The tea restores 2d8+5 Hit Points, and you reduce the DC of [recovery checks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2326) by 1 for 8 hours.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The tea reduces the DC of recovery checks by 3.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

_Spiritsight tea_ exudes a soft blue glow, creating illumination equal to that of a candle. When consumed, your eyes take on a soft glow as well, and you can see [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) creatures and objects for 10 minutes; such creatures appear to you as translucent shapes, and they’re [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) to you. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures.

---

**Activate—Tea Ceremony** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The duration of being able to see invisible creatures and objects increases to 30 minutes, and the duration of the item bonus increases to 8 hours.

Frightened animals depicted on this fulu flee in all directions from a central figure (traditionally represented by a human hunter, but sometimes depicted as a skeletal [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature or even a [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) with long, broken arms). If this causes the [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) to miss, you become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) from the triggering creature until the start of your next turn.

This fulu shows a series of constellations and arrows depicting astronomical movements through the night sky. When you activate a _traveler’s fulu_, the magic infuses your mind with sensations of deja vu, as if you’d been in this region before. Your attempt to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) functions as if you have a compass, and you use the outcome one degree of success better than the result of your [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) check to Sense Direction, or by two degrees of success if you’re a master in Survival.
